payload.description: Scrub SUPER_HARNESS_* from the verification subprocess env so an exported CHANGE_ID cannot poison e2e tests (HG-ENV-LEAK)

payload.reason: Two independent reviewers (Claude subagent + Codex read-only cross-review), converged after 1 fix round. R1: both CHANGES-REQUESTED on Task 3 acceptance bypassing the runner (bare pytest never hits the scrub); fixed to route via 'verify --check pytest' with bare pytest as negative control. Also narrowed design boundary-2 prose + aligned plan File Structure with in-scope docs. R2 Codex: APPROVE, code route verified, no new defects.

Two independent reviewers (Claude subagent + Codex read-only), both APPROVE. Claude empirically reverted the base to raw os.environ and confirmed the regression test genuinely fails on old code (coupled, not tautology). Fixed in-scope NITs: run_check contract docstring, _scrubbed_environ knob breadth, baseline git-diff invariant comment. One out-of-scope cosmetic NIT (verification_config.py:42) deferred to OPEN-ITEMS.

findings.message: engineering/verification_config.py:42 module docstring still describes the exec-time merge as raw `os.environ + defaults.env + check.env`. Incidental (describes load-vs-exec layer separation, not merged_env construction), and out of this change's scope; fixing it triggers the no-redeclare reslug tax. Deferred to OPEN-ITEMS: update to "scrubbed os.environ" next time that file is touched.
